name: White Chocolate Creme Brulee
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces White imported chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 300°F.
2. Whisk egg yolks and 1/2 cup sugar in a medium bowl until combined.
3. Bring cream and remaining 1/2 cup sugar to a simmer in a heavy saucepan over low heat.
4. Gradually add chopped chocolate to the hot cream mixture, whisking constantly until smooth.
5. Gradually whisk the hot chocolate mixture into the egg yolk mixture, stirring continuously.
6. Mix in vanilla extract.
7. Pour the custard into four 10-ounce custard cups (or creme brulee cups).
8. Place the cups in a large baking pan and add enough hot water to the pan to come halfway up the sides of the cups.
9. Bake until the custards are set in the center, about 1 hour.
10. Remove the custards from the water and let cool completely.
11. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
12. Preheat the broiler.
13. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons sugar over each chilled custard.
14. Broil until the sugar caramelizes, watching carefully for about 2 minutes.
15. Serve immediately or refrigerate up to 1 hour and serve cold.
